steps.observed: Detail view opened with heading 'exp_lme_0008', status badge 'accepted_cv', and '← Back to Leaderboard' button

steps.observed: Detail view opened with heading 'exp_lme_0063', status badge 'validation_failed', validation error message displayed, all metrics show N/A

steps.expected: Detail view opens with exp_lme_0059 displayed prominently
steps.observed: Detail view opened with heading 'exp_lme_0059', status badge 'improved', full metrics displayed

assertions.title: Detail View Back Navigation

steps.action: Navigate to exp_lme_0059 detail view with era=LongMemEval and search=exp_lme_0059
Click '← Back to Leaderboard' in-app button
steps.expected: Return to leaderboard with era=LongMemEval and search=exp_lme_0059 preserved
steps.observed: Returned to leaderboard. Era=LongMemEval preserved, search text=exp_lme_0059 preserved, filtered results showing exp_lme_0059 and exp_lme_0060

steps.action: Navigate to exp_lme_0068 detail view, then press browser back button
steps.expected: Browser back button returns to leaderboard
steps.observed: Browser back button had no effect. Detail view remained open. The detail view is a same-page overlay (not a separate route), so there is no browser history entry to navigate back to.

assertions.issues: Browser back button does not work because the detail view is implemented as a same-page overlay (Dash dcc.Store toggle), not as a separate URL route. The validation contract specifies 'Browser back button also returns to leaderboard' but this is architecturally impossible with the current same-page overlay approach. The in-app back button works correctly and preserves all filter state.

steps.observed:

steps.action: Open exp_lme_0008 detail view and verify hypothesis text against hypothesis.txt
steps.expected: Full hypothesis.txt text displayed, not truncated
steps.observed: Full hypothesis text displayed verbatim: 'Sharpen the sigmoid floor transition while keeping the successful exp_lme_0002 floor height fixed. Because LongMemEval answer-bearing memories carry higher impact than distractors, increasing sigmoid_k from 20 to 30 should keep target memories near the same floor while pushing low-importance distractors closer to zero floor. The intended outcome is better ranking selectivity and threshold separation without the broad recall loss seen when floor_max was reduced globally.' — matches hypothesis.txt exactly

steps.action: Verify all params.json key-value pairs displayed in structured format
steps.expected: All 12 params shown as key-value pairs
steps.observed: All 12 params displayed: alpha=1.5, floor_max=0.45, jost_power=4.0, lambda_episode=0.035, lambda_fact=0.008, reinforcement_gain_assoc=0.3, reinforcement_gain_direct=0.4, sigmoid_k=30.0, sigmoid_mid=0.3, stability_cap=2.0, stability_decay=0.005, stability_weight=1.0 — matches params.json exactly

steps.action: Check exp_lme_0063 detail view for parameters section with missing files
steps.observed: exp_lme_0063 has params.json, all 13 params displayed correctly (including activation_weight=0.3)

frictions.description: Clicking a table row toggles selection state (select/deselect). If a row is already selected, clicking it again deselects it and does NOT open the detail view. To open the detail view for an already-selected row, you must first deselect (by clicking elsewhere or pressing Escape), then click the row again. This is because Dash DataTable's row click both fires cellClicked (opens detail) AND toggles selectedRows (which can fire on_row_click with empty array if deselecting).
frictions.resolution: Used page reload to clear selection state before clicking rows. The first click always works; only subsequent clicks on the same or another previously-selected row exhibit this toggle behavior.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 4 passed, 1 failed (VAL-TABLE-010: browser back button does not work because detail view is a same-page overlay, not a separate route). All key metrics, conditional metrics, hypothesis text, and parameter display verified correct against source files.
